lyrics:
Lay down your gun
Your end is near
It's time to run
Away from here
Leave the regrets
You can't ignore
Mercy rejects
What you lied for

The bridge gets you over
The rage of the river
The loss of a lover
The subtle surrender

You will never remember
Unless you dare to go there
Crossing with a certain sense…
Of uncertainty

Step from the flame
Before it knows
How much of your shame
The father owns
Count what you kept
And what you sold
The promise wept
Before it grew cold

The bridge gets you over
The rage of the river
The loss of a lover
The subtle surrender

You will never remember
Unless you dare to go there
Crossing with a certain sense…
Of uncertainty

The bridge gets you over
The rage of the river
The loss of a lover
The subtle surrender

You will never remember
Unless you dare to go there
Crossing with a certain sense…
Of uncertainty

Crossing with a certain sense of uncertainty

impression.text: This track establishes a dramatic and anthemic atmosphere, building from a spacious, clean guitar introduction into a dense, powerful hard rock arrangement. The energy is dynamic and driving, with a powerful rhythm section and soaring vocals creating a sense of forward momentum and epic scale. The production is modern and polished, featuring a wide stereo image where layered guitars and powerful drums create a full wall of sound, while still allowing the lead vocal to cut through clearly. A defining characteristic is the powerful transition into the chorus at 1:02, which unleashes the full energy of the arrangement and solidifies the track's arena-rock feel, further emphasized by the virtuosic guitar solos in the latter half.

review.review_text: Rose builds this one like a preacher who also plays arenas: the opening comes in clean and spacious, then the full arrangement drops at the chorus around the one-minute mark and the room gets noticeably bigger. F# major at a clip just over 160 BPM gives the whole thing a restless, forward-leaning energy that suits the lyric perfectly, 'crossing with a certain sense of uncertainty' being the kind of line that sounds obvious until you realize it's the truest thing on the track. The guitar work in the back half earns its space, and the chorus hook lands with the same chest-forward confidence you get from peak-era Tom Petty. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'crossing with a certain sense of uncertainty'
clean guitar intro into full arrangement
